quotes.quote: The area was just full of people, golfers everywhere, so I was well aware I had quite an audience when I stepped onto the first tee. Being a fairly new member and only an average golfer I felt quite intimidated by the number of people round about.

I settled over the ball, trying to compose myself, took an almighty swish with my driver and thinned the ball straight along the ground... the ball travelled a good 30 yards about 6 inches off the ground before hitting, square on, the ladies tee marker. The ball was propelled into the air and in what seemed like slow motion arced backwards over my head and landed in a bush at the back of the teeing area.

Needless to say, the laughter was both spontaneous and raucous. Somehow, I managed to stammer out 'I'll declare that unplayable and take three of the tee'. I really cannot remember how the second tee shot went, I was probably too traumatised!

2nd tournament I've ever played in, member-guest at my buddies' club. No 'crowd' but probably 10-12 random people watching (near a refreshment stand). Been working toward this tourney for a while, playing with my buddy who is a 2.

It was the wrong green. I had hit the wrong way off the teebox to the green of the par 5 2nd hole. He had been busy and hadn't seen me laser the wrong hole. We had no idea what to do, rules wise, so I sheepishly picked up my ball.
The playing partners we were with that day were still laughing. One of them said my fist pump after the swing was the funniest part.

Once only, thank the lord, i hit my drive into 'negative' territory. On the tee, loaded up the driver and let loose... i drove the club head into the turf behind the ball and popped the ball 10 yards into the air and 10 yards behind the tee box. Now, rather than 380 yards to the green i had 390 to go.
Well, all i could do was to chuckle a bit, accept the humiliation and get on with the next shot. That's about the most memorable ugly tee shot for me.
I felt a lot of dread going over to the tee box to play my shot....that's for sure!! I was a 1-2HC at that time......I definitely had to swallow my pride to go play that recovery shot.
Anyone who plays regularly has many embarrassing stories to share...LOL This topic has been covered before and I've shared this story before.
we catched up 4 girls in front of us, colourful clothes, pretty cute and they gave us a way, so we tee up, hit a really good drive, wowed them. Then we use our cart passed the ladies tee, act cool
I havent play on that course again since that day
I was really nailing my shots to the point that I actually started missing a few on purpose because otherwise I would look too good and blow the joke before we even teed off.

First hole, before the club house. People sitting on the terrace watching. The green was 20 yards lower then the tee box... So I was pretty nervous and I blew up the first hole and the second and the third, fourth. Well you get the picture. It was four hours of the purest frustration.
And at the end I told no one I took lessons and actually trained pretty serious. It took me three years to come clean about it.

One of my first rounds playing and standing on the Par 3, I picked my 9i out of the bag (thinking it was a 6i). Hit it as well as I could and was delighted with myself as I grabbed my tee from the ground. Looked up to see my playing partners looking on quite puzzled.... I turned my attention back to the ball and watched as it landed 35 yards short....

An embarrassing walk followed as we all laughed on our way to the ball. 'The best thing about it was the sheer delight on your face and your newbie cockiness as you reached for the tee.... we knew it was short as soon as you hit it'

It was the first day of golf tryouts last year, and I was paired with a new freshman who ended up winning conference and another good golfer I knew... I step up with a 3-wood and proceed to hit the most dangerous shot known to worm-kind: it skidded on the ground, not even making it to the ladies tees. All this in front of the new golf coach and the new player on the team.
the new player and coach came up toe surprised that I shot a 78. They both apparently didn't think I broke 90 after that tee shot and the triple!

So many to choose from but the one that stands out the most with me was when I was a junior in a fairly competitive tournament years ago. I was on the 18th green with family, friends and spectators aside waiting and watching.

I had previously missed a 10ft putt leaving it short about 6 inches and I let my opponents know I would finish out. I stepped up, made the stroke and stubbed the putter on the ground before contacting the ball, leaving it short about 2 inches. It was the cherry on top to an already dreadful day

I was a bit of a hot-head on the course when I was a kid and made it a really bad habit of throwing and/or breaking clubs when I'd hit a bad shot. And since that happened more often than I'd have preferred - that happened a lot.
Looking back, those shows of immaturity is definitely embarrassing. My dad and my coaches were 100% right in burning my ass for it

The guy hits a wild shot with his 3W off the tee; I don't even remember where it went. But this guy (he was a friend at the time) goes ballistic. Slamming his club, using every known swear word, throwing kicks at some imaginary... something. He finishes it off by throwing his club into a nearby tree.
The rest of us are just watching. I can't speak for the others, but I was beyond embarrassed. For him, and for us being associated with it.

One time when I was 12, I was playing with several random adults while on vacation... I slid about 10 feet down the slope on my stomach, in front of the 3 adults that I was playing with, struggled to get back up the slope, and had to play the rest of the round with a huge grass stain across the entire front of my shirt and pants.

Shortly after he showed me some basics, he started calling me an idiot and that I needed to pay attention to what he said. I put up with this for about a half an hour as his verbal abuse was escalating to an unbelievable level.
where I never liked Golf to begin with, I never did and never will attempt to learn the game again
It doesn't matter if you suck as long as you suck fast. No one wants to be waiting around for the amateur pretending to be Tiger Wood.
Take out your nine iron and try to hit the fucking thing so the next guy can go and all those people behind you waiting in their carts can go.

You really see people how they are - golf brings it out. I have seen people on the course who, from watching them play, I know I want to get closer to in the office because they're the kind of people, no matter how businesslike they are, who will help you

you discover who the real 'gentlemen' are during the game, the people who are brusque at the office, or all business, but who will take a minute to tell you how to turn your club to improve your shot, or who are philosophical when they miss an easy put and humble when they make that great shot
A guy who is cheating on the course is probably cheating on his wife and cheating the expense account, and stabbing his friends in the back in the office. Character tells.

On day on a dog leg left par five hole I had driven my ball about 300 yards and decided to use a 3 iron to cut the corner over a heavily treed hazard. I pulled it off and my ball landed on the green. Feeling smug and invincible I told her, 'Let's go.'

I noticed that he was trying to hit the ball really hard so I explained that a smooth swing that makes good contact will hit the ball a lot farther than a hard swing because the contact will be better and offered to demonstrate.

Turns out I hit what may have been the most pure shot in the history of golf and with that wind behind me, the ball (hit way up in the air with a 7-wood) easily cleared the end of the range which is around 325 yards long. It took several minutes for him to stop laughing.
On about hole 11 of a tournament, I bent down to read a putt and was greeted to a loud ripping sound as my pants split apart.

Once finished I skipped the awards ceremony and tried to slink my way out the parking lot and did so pretty successfully. Once I got home, I was horrified to see that the split went from mid-thigh all the way up my backside. The opening was huge.
I have a lot of respect for the guys that played with me that day who didn't even bother to tease me. But yeah, nobody wants to see that.

Wasn't on the course. It was on the driving range. I was hitting 3 woods. I almost whiffed, but barely hit the heel. The ball came out nearly 90 degrees, barely missing my front leg. It was not moving very fast, so it dribbled to a stop right in front of the guy hitting right behind me.

180yd par three end up in the sand to the left of the green. No problem I tend to be ok out of green side bunkers. This time I for whatever reason pick the ball clean out of the bunker and bounce it off of 2 cars in the parking lot about 75 yards away before it continues to roll down the street and into someone's front yard.
Par 3, 3rd hole at Blackmoor GC in Richmond, Ohio. I reversed shanked the ball. I hit the outside of the ball with the outside of the hosel. The ball bounced and hit me.
being able to play golf is as good as having an MBA; being able to play good golf is as good as having an MBA from Harvard.

So my worst moment was followed by my best.... I chipped my approach, one bounce and straight in the hole.... As it dropped in, I turned to the group and said, I got the right club that time. Think it was my first birdie too!
Drive for show, putt for dough.
